title: AI Swarm Simulator
creator: Agent Buildprint
stack: Graph data model and canvas interactions
summary: AI Swarm Simulator Buildprint remapped from MiroFish as a social simulation workbench with scenario intake, ontology review, a real graph canvas workbench, simulation runtime monitor, prediction report, and interaction console.
plainDescription: A source-independent executable Buildprint remapped from MiroFish as a local-first AI swarm/social simulation workbench. It now uses the review/handover v4 packet shape instead of obsolete proof-ledger theater.
promise: Build a credible AI swarm/social simulation workbench without depending on the original source repo: project intake, graph memory, canvas inspection, simulation runtime, report generation, interaction, persistence, and honest provider/runtime blockers.
whatYouGet: Executable Buildprint packet with BUILDPRINT.md canonical start
A generated agent prompt that frames the task as senior product/design alignment, not checklist compliance
Four feature phases from scenario intake through report interaction plus an explicit final review/handover phase
Explicit graph-workbench requirements: meaningful nodes/edges, selection, inspectors, layout, pan/zoom, refresh, and downstream simulation effects
Anti-slop final reviewer requirements for dead controls, placeholders, canned outputs, weak graph substitutes, and non-persistent state
whatYouNeed: LLM provider credentials for live ontology/profile/report proof, or explicit blocked-provider evidence
Zep-compatible graph-memory credentials for live graph proof, or a deterministic sandbox adapter clearly labeled as such
OASIS/CAMEL-compatible runtime or approved local deterministic simulation worker
Browser/e2e environment for graph interaction, persistence, report, and console proof
includes: Scenario intake and ontology review phase
Graph canvas workbench phase with explicit no-token-bubbles constraint
Simulation preparation and runtime monitor phase
Prediction report and interaction console phase
Final critical review and handover phase
risks: Collapsing the graph workbench into token bubbles, chips, cards, or static SVG decoration
Canned ontology/report/simulation outputs that do not change with inputs
Dead buttons or fake controls presented as completed product behavior
Counting deterministic sandbox adapters as live provider proof
Omitting provider adapters because credentials are missing
Raw debug/admin surfaces standing in for product UX
Secret leakage through logs, reports, screenshots, or evidence rows
checks: generated/agent-prompt.md is alignment speech and includes anti-slop review
Graph canvas phase explicitly forbids token bubbles/static SVG/card-list substitutes
Final critical reviewer checks dead buttons, placeholders, canned outputs, persistence, and graph-workbench quality

trustBadges.label: Executable packet
trustBadges.detail: The remapped packet passes the Mapper executable-integrity check.

trustBadges.label: Graph workbench protected
trustBadges.detail: The central artifact must be meaningful graph/canvas behavior, not token bubbles or static decoration.

trustBadges.label: Anti-slop review
trustBadges.detail: Final handover requires a harsh product-quality review after build/test/lint proof.

copyPrompt: Use the AI Swarm Simulator Buildprint. First bootstrap exact snapshots: agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/ai-swarm-simulator/package.json . If agb is not installed, clone https://github.com/DomEscobar/agent-buildprint and run node agent-buildprint/bin/agb.js start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/ai-swarm-simulator/package.json . Then read .buildprint/next-agent.md and continue. Do not write Buildprint snapshots manually. The bootstrapped packet is the source of truth — follow its read order: BUILDPRINT.md, 01-questions.md, generated/agent-prompt.md, 02-project-setup.md, blueprint.yaml, 03-phases/phase-index.yaml, 03-phases/phase-flow.md, 04-review.md, 05-handover.md, and the active phase file.

publicStatus.label: v4 review/handover packet
publicStatus.explanation: Bootstraps through the current executable packet shape with 04-review.md and 05-handover.md; live providers remain honest blockers when unavailable.
